celebration: Sam joins the cast — the peg-doll boy, Segina's friend

songs.title: S-S-Snake
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
S! S! S-s-snake!
(ssssss… ssssss…)

[Hook]
S! S! S-s-s!
What begins with S?
S-s-snake! S-s-snake!
It's a snake! (a snake!)

[Verse 2]
S! S! S-s-s!
What begins with S?
S-s-sun! S-s-sun!
It's a sun! (a sun!)

[Verse 3]
S! S! S-s-s!
What begins with S?
S-s-sock! S-s-sock!
It's a sock! (a sock!)

[Verse 4]
S! S! S-s-s!
What begins with S?
S-s-star! S-s-star!
It's a star! (a star!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not S!
(ssssss… ssssss…)

[Outro]
S-s-snake! S-s-snake!
It's a snake! (a snake!)
S! S! S-s-s!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The S sound is a long steady hiss /sss/ like a snake — NEVER 'ess' (letter name) and NEVER 'suh' (schwa). The '(ssssss…)' lines are a sustained snake hiss riding the 808. Answer line 'It's a ___!' reuses the Week 1 frame.

songs.title: Sam Sat
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
(Sam… Sam… Sam sat…)

[Hook]
Sam sat! Sam sat!
Where did Sam sit?
Sam sat on the mat! (the mat!)
Sam sat on the mat!

[Verse 2]
Sam sat! Sam sat!
Where did Sam sit?
Sam sat on the sock! (a sock?!)
Sam sat on the sock!

[Verse 3]
Sam sat! Sam sat!
Where did Sam sit?
Sam sat on the… CAT! (the cat?!)
Sam sat on the cat! (oh no!)

[Bridge]
The cat sat on Sam! (on Sam!)
The cat sat on Sam!

[Outro]
Sam sat on the mat.
The cat sat on the mat.
(shhh…)
Sam sat on a POTATO!
(PO-TA-TO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Sing the boy's name as 'Sam' (as spelled). One frame: 'Sam sat on the ___.' — a single word swaps per verse. Verse 3 is the book's gag (Sam sits on the cat), the bridge is the cat's revenge. Kids choir answers the (…?!) echoes; whisper the outro, then the potato hits loud.

spreads.artNote: Sam's grand entrance — a small wooden peg-doll boy with painted brown hair and blue dungarees, spotlit, cheerful. Cast member joins beside Segina and the Cat.
Sam plops straight down, happy, legs out — the whole book is Sam sitting on things.
Sam sitting neatly in the middle of the familiar woven mat. All is well… for now.

spreads.text: A cat is on the mat!
spreads.artNote: The tabby cat is already curled on the mat — Sam hasn't noticed. Uh-oh setup.
THE GAG. Sam has sat right down on top of the cat, oblivious and beaming; the cat's eyes bulge, tail rigid, one paw sticking out.
The flip — the cat perches smugly on Sam's head like a hat, fair is fair. Children READ the reversal.
Both sit side by side on the mat now, arms crossed, sulking but calm — a truce.

spreads.text: Sam is on the mat. The cat is on the mat.
spreads.artNote: Peace at last — Sam and the cat share the mat, Segina peeking in approvingly from the edge.
The potato has appeared on the mat beside them. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. Sam and the cat stare, deadpan.

sentences: The cat is on the mat.
extras: Moveable alphabet: build s-a-t, S-a-m, c-a-t-s, m-a-t-s with sandpaper letters s, a, t, m, c
Sandpaper letter 's' + sand tray — trace the long hiss
/s/ sound basket + 8 sound cards (snake, sun, sock, star, soap, seal, saw, sandwich)
Musical-chairs 'Sam Sat' sitting game — freeze on the beat
Sam wooden peg-doll boy added to the figures basket beside Segina

teacherFocus: Introduce the /s/ hiss (the snake sound — long and steady, never 'suh'). Blend s-a-t → sat, and meet Sam: the peg-doll boy who sits on everything. Sam is Segina's counterpart — a boy the boys can point to. Play musical chairs to 'Sam Sat', freezing on the beat, and let the class read the cat-and-Sam sitting gag aloud. The whole book is one frame — 'Sam sat on the ___.' — so once they crack line one they read the rest themselves.
